Headline: Financial - Private School Openings Outpace Closures, England
Info: A new analysis of Department for Education records reveals that 38 private schools have opened and 10 have closed so far this year, indicating that overall sector growth continues despite the financial pressures of the recent VAT introduction.

Headline: Financial - Immanuel College, Hertfordshire
Info: Following severe financial challenges and falling enrolment that threatened closure, Immanuel College has been saved after a parent-led campaign raised £12m in nine days, leading to a rescue deal and the appointment of a new chair.

Headline: Closure - Trinity Christian School, Reading
Info: Trinity Christian School permanently closed its doors recently due to financial difficulties exacerbated by the introduction of VAT on private education, leaving some pupils with special educational needs without a suitable state placement.

Headline: Closure - Malvern St James School, Worcestershire
Info: Malvern St James School has announced its proposed closure, driven by a significant decline in pupil numbers and broader financial pressures facing the independent sector.

Headline: Closure - Abertillery Learning Community (State), Blaenau Gwent
Info: The local council is consulting on the potential closure of the Roseheyworth campus of the Abertillery Learning Community to address surplus spaces and a projected £4 million financial deficit by the 2029/30 academic year.

Headline: Closure - Malvern St James School, Worcestershire
Info: Following the recent announcement of its planned closure at the end of the academic year, local commentary has underscored the severe financial pressures and declining pupil numbers impacting the independent school.
